# DarkSlide v1.0.0

DarkSlide 1.0.0 is the first stable release of the film negative conversion workspace. It focuses on a more faithful conversion core, inspectable output, safer color handling, and production-ready desktop builds for macOS, Windows, and Linux.

## Highlights

- Density-domain negative inversion now drives conversions from a film-base sample, with symmetric flare subtraction and a better-behaved black point.
- Deterministic conversion parameters are pinned per document so preview, single export, batch export, and diagnostics can agree on the same result.
- Embedded matrix/TRC ICC input profiles are parsed directly with D50 to D65 adaptation, reducing unwanted fallback to sRGB.
- PNG and TIFF export flows now expose bit-depth controls, with graceful fallback when a requested high-depth path is not available.
- Batch export now uses the same color-versus-monochrome routing as preview and single export.
- Export buttons now show clearer busy states and avoid duplicate/ghosted labels during active exports.

## Desktop Downloads

GitHub Actions builds and attaches installers for each supported desktop platform:

- macOS: universal Apple Silicon and Intel `.dmg`
- Windows: `.msi` and NSIS `.exe`
- Linux: `.deb` and `.AppImage`

The desktop app adds RAW import, native dialogs, file associations, scanning sessions, external editor handoff, and local update checks. The browser app remains available for JPEG, PNG, TIFF, and WebP workflows without installing anything.

## Conversion And Color Pipeline

- Added the density-domain inversion path with wired density balance.
- Made conversion analysis deterministic and inspectable across render and export requests.
- Preserved stale-render protection through request revisioning while tightening export consistency.
- Improved TIFF IFD selection and mirrored-orientation handling.
- Removed the obsolete `white_balance` pipeline field.
- Added parsed ICC input profile support for embedded matrix/TRC profiles.
- Fixed embedded PNG ICC profile zlib framing so exported profiles round-trip correctly in other applications.

## Export Improvements

- Added bit-depth export controls and preset normalization.
- Added 16-bit RAW export support where the pipeline can supply high-depth data.
- Preserved PNG compression for 8-bit PNG output.
- Added a warning/fallback path when 16-bit export is requested but only 8-bit output can be produced.
- Fixed batch color-pipeline selection so black-and-white toggles are honored consistently.
- Polished export button states during long-running exports.

## Known Notes

- macOS builds are not notarized. On first launch, macOS may require opening the app through System Settings > Privacy & Security > Open Anyway.
- Windows and Linux builds are unsigned.
- RAW support is available in the desktop app and remains an advanced workflow; JPEG, PNG, TIFF, and WebP are the stable browser-supported formats.

## Checks

This release is built by GitHub Actions on macOS, Windows, and Linux. The release workflow also keeps the generated installers as workflow artifacts so they can be inspected even if a release upload has to be retried.
